title: Rosa × nicholsonii

This rose species is further classified into the section Caninae and subsection Caninae. Rosa × nicholsonii is thought to be a hybrid of Rosa canina and Rosa gallica. The classification of this species is still a matter of ongoing research and debate among botanists.

intro: Rosa × nicholsonii, also known as the Nicholson's rose, is a hybrid species of rose that is native to the United Kingdom. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in reducing symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. The bioactive constituents of Rosa × nicholsonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
